title: The One With The Tea Leaves

lines.text: The Hallway, Rachel walks up the stairs and knocks on Joey's door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey? Are you in there?
[Cut to inside Monica and Chandler's, Monica, Chandler and Joey are eating breakfast.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (hearing Rachel and jumping up with his plate) Oh God! That's Rachel!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey, you have to talk to her!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no, I can't! I can't! Not after the other night, it's just it's...too weird, okay? Don't tell her I'm here! (Turns to run to the bathroom and his bagel falls off the plate onto the floor.) Don't eat that! (Runs to the bathroom as Rachel enters.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Is Joey here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't see him. (To Monica) Do you see him?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't see him. Hey! Maybe he's in the sugar bowl! (Opens the sugar bowl) Joey? Nope! (Closes the sugar bowl and they both laugh.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, at least you make each other laugh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I haven't seen him since that night that he told me how he y'know... I don't know, I think he's avoiding me. Why is that bagel on the floor?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We were playing a game.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ew, was Chandler naked? Sort of like a, like a ring toss kind of situation?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?! No! No!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right. Well listen, if you see Joey will you just tell him uh...tell him I miss him. (Exits and Joey enters.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (To Joey) Okay, did you hear that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, a naked bagel game? (Picks up his dropped bagel.) (To Chandler) Dude, I don't know. That's a pretty small hole.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, you gotta talk to her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't! Y'know? You guys don't know what it's like to put yourself out there like that and just get shot down.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (incredulous) I don't know what that's like?! Up until I was 25 I thought the only response to, "I love you," was, "Oh crap!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hello? No rejection? I got shot down at fat camp! Boy, kids are mean when they're hungry.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right so, so what do I do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This is Rachel. I mean, what are you gonna do, never going to talk to her again? I mean I know it's weird, it's awkward, but you gotta at least try.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah. Okay. (Goes to take a bite out of the previously mentioned bagel.) Whoa! (Stops.) I almost forgot this was on your...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (interrupting him) We didn't play it!!
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Phoebe is holding a book as she, Rachel, and Monica drink some tea as Chandler looks on.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, so when you're done with your tea I'll look at your leaves and tell you your fortune.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't know you read tea leaves.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, I've done it for years. I actually stopped because I was so accurate. Y'know, and-and y'know, one of the great joys of life is it's-it's wondrous unpredictability. Y'know? And also tea tends to give me the trots.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I'm done. Read mine.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Looks at the leaves.) Ooh, I see a ladder. (Checks the book) Which can mean either a promotion or a violent death.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (stunned) I-I'm the head chef. I-I can't get promoted.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-hmm. Uh-hmm, who's next?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, I'm done. Do mine.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. (Reads the leaves) Umm, oh! Okay, I see a circle.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! (Checking the book) Which can either mean you're having a baby or you're gonna make a scientific discovery!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I have been spending a lot of time in the lab.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What does yours say Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm... Wow, all right. (Checks the book.) Wow! Yay! Ooh, I'm gonna meet a guy! And really soon! And he's gonna be the man of my dreams. Probably not the guy I had a dream about last night. (Points at Chandler.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey! Has anyone seen my shirt? It's a button down, like a, like a faded salmon?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You mean your pink shirt?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Faded salmon color.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, I-I haven't seen your pink shirt.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Great! Great. Then I must've left it at Mona's. I knew it!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I'm sure you get another one at Ann Taylor's.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's my favorite shirt! Okay? I love that shirt!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well just ask Mona to give it back!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know. I mean I-I guess I could. It's just that we didn't really end things such good terms. And if I go over there I'd be ignoring the one thing she asked me to do when we broke up, jump up my own ass and die. (Walks away.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (noticing a guy sitting by the green post looking at her) Oh wait a second you guys...for the last couple weeks I've been that guy everywhere I go. We take the same bus. We go to the same bookstore, the same dry cleaners; maybe he's the tea guy. (He gets up to leave, and smiles at Phoebe.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe, did you see that?! He totally checked you out! He is so cute! (Looking at his tea.) Mine has a picture of The Village People, what does that mean?
Joey enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.
He slowly walks to the other side of the couch and sits down at the table, an awkward silence follows.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi.
More awkward silence.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Tea gives Phoebe the trots.
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is cooking as Rachel paces nervously.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So I thought Joey and I would be okay once we hung out, but it's not even like we know how to be with each other anymore.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know it's tough now, but things will get better.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How do you know that? What if it just gets worse and worse and worse, to the point where we can't even be in the same room with each other?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not great at the advice, can I interest you in a sarcastic comment? Some cheese?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, what is the Bruce Springsteen CD in the Kat Stevens case?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let's just say if I can't find the right CD case I just put it in the nearest one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, where is the Kat Stevens CD?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: In the James Taylor case.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Where is the James Taylor CD?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, I'm gonna save you some time, 200 CDs, not one of them in the right case.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. No need to panic. Deep breathes everyone. Okay umm uh, we're just gonna have to spend some time and put the CDs in the right cases.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, if we're gonna do that we should come up with some kind of order. Y'know alphabetically or by genre?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hmm, I don't know. We really have to talk this through.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God!! You guys have such problems!! I feel so terrible for you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I-I'm sorry. You and Joey, your both focusing on this uncomfortable thing, what you need to do is to change the subject. Next time you see him try to get him talking about something else.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah. That makes sense.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, like I don't know, maybe you have a work problem that you need his advice on.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, I can do that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Good. (To Chandler) Uh honey, the Miami Vice soundtrack? Really?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They were just giving those away at the store (off Monica's look) in exchange for money.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh I really don't know what to tell you Rach, I really don't. I mean, maybe Joey can help you out with your, with your big work problem.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah Joey she's...Rachel's got this really big work problem, and it is a head scratcher. Wow! (To Chandler) Y'know what, if we're gonna make dinner we're gonna have to leave. Yeah. (She and Chandler exit.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So you uh, have a...big work problem?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah it's umm... Yeah it's uh... It-it's y'know-It's nothing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh. Okay. (Awkward silence.) So uh, I think I'm gonna take off.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah-No wait! Joey no wait it is. It's something. It's-it's umm...it's my boss.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, and umm my baby.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My boss wants to buy my baby!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! Oh my-oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know I told you, it's a really big problem.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What he wants to buy your baby?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Can you believe that?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's crazy!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's what I told him!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, how did this even happen?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well I'll tell ya! (Pause) See uh my-my boss and his wife-They-they can't have children. So umm, and that-we were at the Christmas party, and he got drunk, and he said to me, "Rachel, I want to buy your baby."

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man! When you said it was a problem about your boss and the baby I figured it was something about maternity leave.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! Yeah! (Pause) Yeah that-that would've been a much simpler problem.
A newsstand, Phoebe is looking at a magazine as the guy from before walks by and picks up a newspaper.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hello.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Oh, it's you. I see you everywhere. I'm Jim, Jim Nelson.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh Jim, Jim Nelson I'm Phoebe, Phoebe Buffay. We certainly have been seeing a lot of each other lately.

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: We have. Maybe we'll be seeing each other at dinner tomorrow night, say around 8 o'clock?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, maybe we will. (Starts to walk away.) Oh! (She turns around and the exchange information.)
Outside Mona's Apartment, Ross is knocking on the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Mona? (There's no answer, so he starts to leave but remembers where her extra key is. He reaches atop a hall light just outside her door and grabs the key. He looks at in triumph as the pain from it being hot moves along his nervous system to his brain, and when it arrives his brain orders his hand to drop the hot key and his mouth to squeal in pain. After dropping the key he pulls his shirtsleeve over his hand and uses the key to open the door and enter Mona's apartment.) Okay, if I were a salmon shirt, where would I be? (He hears a key in the door and as it opens he dives behind the couch.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: (entering, with her date) I am so sorry I spilled wine all over your shirt.

lines.speaker: Mona's Date
lines.text: Oh, it's okay.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: No, it's still wet. Y'know what? Let me get it out before it sets. Ooh, I have something you can wear. Here. (Hands him Ross's shirt.)

lines.speaker: Mona's Date
lines.text: Oh umm, I-I don't know if I want to wear a woman's shirt.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: No-no that's a man's shirt.

lines.speaker: Mona's Date
lines.text: It's awfully pink. (Ross mouths, "It's salmon!")
Monica and Chandler's, Monica and Chandler are sorting their CDs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God, honey we are so meant to be together. We both have copies of the Annie soundtrack.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Honey, both yours.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey! Great advice on that Joey thing!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah? The work problem?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh it was perfect! I mean it really felt like he was my friend again.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What problem did you tell him you had?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh that's not important. The point is, I really-I think everything's gonna be okay.
Mr. Zelner's Office, he's in a meeting as Joey bursts in.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: May I help you?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you think you can just buy my friends baby?!
Commercial Break
A restaurant, Phoebe and Jim are on their date.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Isn't it funny how we kept running into each other? It's as if someone really wants us to be together.

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: Someone does. Me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, witty banter. Well done.

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: So, tell me a little bit about yourself.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh okay, well I'm a masseuse, and I used to work at this place...

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: Do you like to party?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I-I like, I like parties.

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: You're wild, aren't ya?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I guess, a little.

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: It ain't no thing, I'm wild too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (a little freaked out) So! Umm, anyway I-I lived in New York, someone wildly I guess, for umm-Well since I was fourteen.

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: I'm sorry. I'm staring. It's just that you have the most beautiful eyes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh stop it.

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: And your breasts! Hmm!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay. Umm look, you're coming on a little strong. But I'm going to give you the benefit of the doubt, because it seems the universe really wants to be together. So, why don't we just start over okay? And you can just tell me about yourself.

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: I write erotic novels, for children.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: They're wildly unpopular.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: Oh also, you might be interested to know that I have a Ph.D.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! You do?

lines.speaker: Jim
lines.text: Yeah, (looks at his crotch) a Pretty Huge...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. (Gets up and walks out.)
Monica and Chandler's, Monica, Chandler, and now Rachel are organizing CDs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (singing) The sun'll come out...tomorrow! Bet...your bottom dollar that tomorrow... (The girls start laughing, and in a deep voice) ...there'll be sun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Oh Joey, honey listen, thank you for talking to my yesterday about that thing with my boss. That really meant a lot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not a problem. Oh, and just so you know, that guy's not going to be bothering you about that baby thing anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (worried) What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Let's just say I took care of it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa, let's say more!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't worry! Don't worry. I just told him, very nicely, "You don't go buying people's babies, so back off!!"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Chandler and Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No, no-no-no Joey he doesn't want to buy my baby! I made that up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What?! Why?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So that we would have something to talk about! So it wouldn't be awkward!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And you couldn't think of anything else?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You said your boss wants to buy your baby?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Joey) I can't believe that you yelled at my boss! I'm-I'm gonna lose my job! What am I going to do?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You can always sell your baby.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Joey, I can't believe you brought my boss into this! I'm gonna get fired!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You lied to me!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, she told me too! (Points to Monica and Joey glares at her.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler has two copies of Annie!

Mona's Apartment, she and her date are making out as Ross flips through a magazine while lying behind the couch and sees something that he likes. Meanwhile, Mona's date takes off Ross's shirt and Mona throws it on the floor. While they start making out again, Ross tries to pull the rug the shirt is on over to him, but while he does that he moves the coffee table and it bumps into the couch.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: (seeing Ross) Oh my God! Ross!!!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hello!!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Ross, what are you doing?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not touching myself if that makes anyone less uncomfortable.
Mr. Zelner's Office, Rachel is knocking on the door.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Morning. You wanted to see me?

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Please, come in. Have a seat. (She does so.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay look Mr. Zelner...

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: Oh I think it's best that I speak first.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. (Motions for him to continue.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: I've asked Lee from human resources to be here as a witness to our conversation.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh God.

lines.speaker: Mr. Zelner
lines.text: If I in any way implied that I wanted to buy your baby...I am sorry. Okay? Last week when I asked you when your due date was uh, I certainly did not mean that I felt that I was due your baby. Yeah, I want to be very clear that I understand that its your baby, and it is not mine to purchase.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, as long as we are clear about that. (Exits smugly.)
A Street, Phoebe and Monica are walking down it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh there it is.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's not your regular dry cleaners.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know, but that creep that I went on that date with goes to there so I have to find a new one. I also have to find a new video store, a new bank, a new adult bookstore, a new grocery store...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A new grocery store. The universe said I was going to meet a nice guy and that's what they gave me? (Looks up) When I get up there I'm going to kick some ass.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't worry Phoebe, you're gonna meet someone. If I can meet a great guy, so can you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, we both can. And we both will.
They enter the dry cleaners.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, you didn't have to come in with me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you kidding? This is where they get out stains! Okay? This is like Disneyland for me. I'm-I'm gonna be over here watching the dance of the clean shirts. (She points to and walks over to the electric clothes rack they have.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.
A guy enters that looks suspiciously like Alec Baldwin from The Hunt for Red October, Pearl Harbor, and Beetlejuice.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Oh, excuse me! I think you dropped s...(looks at Phoebe.) Wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: I'm sorry, it's just that you're so incredibly beautiful.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah well, I'm sorry about that too, but what are you going to do?

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: I hope you don't think I'm crazy but I feel like I was meant to pick this up, do you believe in that kind of thing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: A little. (She turns around and looks up.) (To the universe) Now you're talking.

lines.speaker: Guy
lines.text: Would you like to go out and have a cup of coffee?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I-I'd love to. Let me just tell my friend.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to the clerk) Ooh, an ink stain! Hey, can I watch how you get this out?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looking around) She must've left.
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler is watching Joey pace nervously as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So? What-what-what happened?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's all gonna be okay. They're just so happy that I'm not suing them that they gave me one extra month paid maternity leave. So long as I understand that the money should not construed as a down payment on this or any other child I should bear.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, Ralph Lauren is really going out of there way to show they're not in the baby buying business.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, can you give us a minute?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh I'm sorry, you're kicking me out of my own living room?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll be in there. (Goes into the bedroom.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Joey, I'm really sorry that I lied to you. I was just trying to make things...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know. I know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It kinda worked. I mean y'know, I don't know about you buy I haven't thought about our thing since all this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey you're right. Yeah, it's kinda been like us again a little bit.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah I know! I miss that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Me too. I mean I...haven't thought at all about how I put myself out there and said all that stuff and how you didn't feel the same way about me and-and how it was really awkward.
Awkward silence.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My gynecologist tried to kill me.
Closing Credits
Ross and Rachel's, Ross is ordering something over the phone from a catalog.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on phone) Item J437-A, color: winterberry. (There's a knock on the door as he hangs up the phone. He answers it to Mona.) Hi umm, listen come here, come in. (She does so.) I'm so-so sorry about yesterday. I-I'm really sorry. It's just that I... (He picks up the pink shirt.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: (interrupting him) Listen Ross, you don't have to apologize. I understand why you were there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Yeah, you still have feelings for me. And-and to be honest, I-I still have feelings for you. And I wish that we can work it out Ross, but we can't. It's too complicated with you and Rachel and the baby, I-I just... It just wasn't meant to be.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (faking starting to cry) Oh God you're right.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Ross, we...we have to be strong. Okay, I-I'm gonna go. (She picks up the shirt Ross has just set down.) Can I? To remember you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (laughs) No. (Takes the shirt back.)
End
